scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of Bio-toilets in mechanised fishing vessels - Gujarat
page_title: PMMSY: Establishment of Bio-toilets in mechanised fishing vessels - Gujarat

details: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Establishment of Bio-toilets in mechanised fishing vessels", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.

Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.

Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.

Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits: Women and ST/SC category beneficiaries: 60% assistance, i.e. ₹30,000/-.
Target Allocation: Benefits will be available as per the category allocation decided by the Government of India.
Contribution is shared: 60% from Government of India, 40% from State Governments.
The benefit is provided only once.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the scheme for establishment of bio-toilets in mechanised fishing vessels?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide hygienic sanitation facilities by supporting the installation of bio-toilets in mechanised fishing vessels.

faqs.question: How much is the unit cost for establishing a bio-toilet under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The unit cost for establishing a bio-toilet in a mechanised fishing vessel is ₹50,000/-.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is given to beneficiaries belonging to the general category?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries from the general category receive 40% assistance, which amounts to ₹20,000/-.

faqs.question: What financial support is available for women and ST/SC category beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Women and ST/SC category beneficiaries receive 60% assistance, which amounts to ₹30,000/-.

faqs.question: Who decides the target categories for receiving benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The target categories for receiving benefits are allocated by the Government of India.

faqs.question: How is the funding for this scheme shared between the central and state governments?
faqs.answer: The Government of India contributes 60%, while State Governments contribute 40% towards the financial assistance.

faqs.question: Can a person avail this scheme more than once?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit under this scheme is available only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Is the amount of financial assistance fixed for all categories?
faqs.answer: No, it varies. General category beneficiaries get ₹20,000/-, while women and ST/SC beneficiaries get ₹30,000/-.

faqs.question: Are the benefits provided directly to all applicants?
faqs.answer: The benefits are provided as per the target category allocation decided by the Government of India.

faqs.question: Does the scheme provide partial or full financial assistance for installation?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides partial financial assistance, covering 40% or 60% of the unit cost depending on the beneficiary category.
